$comment: D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-TERMINATION-LAST-REACHABLE (TF-C40, SQLite testfixture-link sub-arc): RUN witness that a non-void function whose body has a trailing null statement `;` AFTER the `return` (the `MACRO(...);` idiom — the macro body ends in `return`, the invocation carries a `;` that lowers to an empty Block as the body's LAST child) COMPILES and RUNS correctly. `pathTerminates` (src/hir/hir_verifier.hpp) now decides a block's termination by its LAST REACHABLE statement, not its literal last child, so the empty trailing Block no longer masks the real `return` terminator. gcc/clang compile this cleanly; before the fix DSS emitted a spurious H0003 (H_VerifierFailure). `f()` returns 42; the dead `;` has no runtime effect. The `release` optimizedPipeline arm witnesses the shipped optimizer over the same source (the dead null statement is pruned). All 4 targets: a front-end/verifier predicate change, target/format-agnostic. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the Block case of pathTerminates to `return !kids.empty() && pathTerminates(src, kids.back())` and this TU no longer COMPILES (H0003).
